comment:
#712 Track 1b — per-camera-model lens calibration. Maps the camera's `hwDescriptor` (as reported by `/api/cameras` from the camera node) to lens-effective constants the orchestrator uses when building floor-coverage polygons. The manufacturer's nominal FOV from the spec sheet is usually 5-15% wider than the lens actually sees once V4L2 cropping + USB-pipeline downsampling are applied; using the nominal value over-claims floor coverage and the cal pipeline aims at cells the camera doesn't actually see.

New rigs inherit these constants automatically by `hwDescriptor` lookup. When a camera's `hwDescriptor` is not in this registry the orchestrator falls back to the fixture's declared `fovDeg` (manufacturer spec) and emits a `cam-fov-no-calibration` warning so the operator knows the polygon is best-effort.

EMEET SmartCam Nova 4K: EMEET S.notes: 4K mode, V4L2-cropped frame. Manufacturer spec is 90 deg diagonal; effective horizontal FOV measured at ~78.5 deg by sighting a 1 m wide target at 0.65 m distance and confirming both edges fall on image columns 0 and 3839 of a 4K frame. k1 barrel distortion estimated visually at +2% on the long edge.
